assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items discuss oil-market moves and Iran-related risks, but do not provide an EIA weekly U.S. retail gasoline price release at/above $4.00/gal. No definitive datapoint confirming or falsifying the threshold crossing is included.
No University of Michigan 1-year inflation expectations (final reading) figure is reported in the provided news bullets.
No citation of the ICE BofA US High Yield Index OAS level (and specifically whether it closed at/above 600 bps) appears in today’s bullets.
No report of Congress enacting a single signed supplemental appropriations law totaling ≥$5.0B specifically for Israel-related air and missile defense is included in today’s bullets.

Multiple headlines indicate the Supreme Court is actively considering a case about late-arriving mail-in ballots, which is within scope. However, the bullets do not clearly state whether the Court (a) granted certiorari or (b) issued an emergency-docket stay/injunction in such a case (the prediction’s specific triggers). Confirmation would require the article details or the relevant SCOTUS docket order.
No mention of CAISO issuing Flex Alerts (or equivalent public conservation/emergency alerts) is present in today’s bullets, and the monitoring window has only just begun.
No quarterly filing/earnings disclosure is cited for the named BDCs showing non-accrual investments at fair value reaching ≥3.0% of total portfolio fair value.
Today’s bullets do not describe a USTR/White House announcement of a new consumer-goods tariff increase of ≥10 percentage points covering ≥$25B in annual import value.
The OpenAI-related news item concerns IPO-related risk disclosures and reliance on Microsoft, not an announcement of a new U.S.-located 500+ MW data-center/compute project.
No information is provided about Saudi Aramco’s month-over-month OSP change for Arab Light to Asia (and specifically a ≥$2.00/bbl increase) in any monthly pricing cycle.
